name: Sprite
description: Sprites, often considered a type of fairy or fey, are ethereal beings primarily associated with water, though they can also be found inhabiting other natural elements like forests, fire, and earth. In folklore, they are typically depicted as mischievous and playful creatures, possessing a deep connection to the natural world. Sprites are often seen as guardians of their specific environment, whether it be a babbling brook, a sun-dappled glade, or a crackling campfire. Their appearance is generally described as small and radiant, with delicate features and wings resembling those of butterflies or dragonflies. They are known for their enchanting songs and dances, which are said to have the power to lure travelers astray or bring good fortune to those who treat them with respect. While generally benevolent, sprites can be easily offended and may retaliate with pranks or misdirection if angered or disrespected. Their existence embodies the inherent magic and wonder found within the natural world.
subtitle: Ethereal spirits of nature.
appearance: Small, radiant beings with delicate features and butterfly-like wings. Often described as shimmering or glowing with an ethereal light. Their clothing, if any, is typically made of natural materials like leaves, flowers, or dewdrops.
habitat: Rivers, streams, forests, meadows, and other natural environments.
behavior: Playful, mischievous, and generally benevolent, but easily offended. They enjoy dancing, singing, and playing pranks on unsuspecting humans. They are fiercely protective of their environment.

keyMyths.title: The Sprite's Gift
keyMyths.description: A kind woodcutter, lost in the forest, offered a Sprite a drink from his flask. The Sprite, grateful for the simple act of kindness, led him to a hidden grove filled with precious stones, ensuring the woodcutter's family would never want for anything. This tale highlights the Sprite's ability to reward kindness.
keyMyths.source: Folk tale from the Black Forest

keyMyths.title: The Misled Traveler
keyMyths.description: A group of arrogant travelers, believing themselves superior, mocked the Sprites they encountered near a river. In retaliation, the Sprites used their illusion magic to lead the travelers in circles, leaving them lost and disoriented until they apologized for their disrespect. This demonstrates the Sprite's vengeful side.

keyMyths.title: The Cleansing of the Stream
keyMyths.description: When a nearby mill began polluting a stream, the Sprites inhabiting it grew weak and listless. A young girl, witnessing their plight, organized the villagers to clean the stream. The Sprites, restored to health, blessed the village with bountiful harvests for years to come. This story emphasizes the Sprite's connection to the environment and their power to influence nature.
keyMyths.source: English Fairy Tales by Joseph Jacobs

sources.author: Briggs, Katharine
